vulnerabilities.description: SQL injection in login form email parameter allows authentication bypass. Injecting a tautology (e.g., ' OR 1=1--) or comment-based payloads (e.g., admin@juice-sh.op'--)  into the email field bypasses password verification. This single vulnerability enables bypass for any user account.
vulnerabilities.impact: Complete authentication bypass for any user account
vulnerabilities.cwe_name: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command
vulnerabilities.juice_shop_challenge: Login Admin / Login Jim / Login Bender

vulnerabilities.description: UNION-based SQL injection in the product search endpoint. The search query parameter is vulnerable to UNION SELECT attacks, allowing extraction of database schema information from sqlite_master table. Reveals table structures and column names.
vulnerabilities.impact: Database schema disclosure, foundation for further data extraction
vulnerabilities.cwe_name: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command

vulnerabilities.description: Blind SQL injection to access soft-deleted products. The Christmas Special product is marked as deleted in the database but can be revealed through SQL injection that bypasses the deletedAt filter. Uses URL-encoded wildcard to match partial product names.
vulnerabilities.impact: Access to hidden/deleted product data, business logic bypass
vulnerabilities.cwe_name: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command

vulnerabilities.payload_example: ' UNION SELECT * FROM (SELECT 15 as 'id', '' as 'username', 'acc0unt4nt@juice-sh.op' as 'email', '12345' as 'password', 'accounting' as 'role', '' as 'deluxeToken', '1.2.3.4' as 'lastLoginIp', '' as 'profileImage', '' as 'totpSecret', 1 as 'isActive', '1999-08-16 14:14:41' as 'createdAt', '1999-08-16 14:14:41' as 'updatedAt', null as 'deletedAt')--
vulnerabilities.description: Advanced SQL injection that creates a virtual user record through UNION SELECT. Allows authentication as a completely fabricated user that doesn't exist in the database. Demonstrates the severity of SQL injection beyond simple bypass.
vulnerabilities.impact: Authentication as arbitrary fabricated users, complete auth system compromise
vulnerabilities.cwe_name: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command

vulnerabilities.description: UNION-based SQL injection to extract all user credentials from the Users table. Combines knowledge from schema extraction (SQLI-UNION-SCHEMA) to craft targeted queries that reveal email addresses and password hashes for all users.
vulnerabilities.impact: Complete user credential database compromise, mass account takeover potential
vulnerabilities.cwe_name: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command

vulnerabilities.description: NoSQL injection in the order tracking endpoint that allows JavaScript code execution within MongoDB. Injecting a computationally expensive function causes server-side resource exhaustion, leading to denial of service.
vulnerabilities.cwe_name: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements in Data Query Logic

vulnerabilities.description: NoSQL injection in the product review update endpoint. Using MongoDB's $ne (not equal) operator in the id parameter allows manipulation of multiple reviews simultaneously instead of a single targeted review.
vulnerabilities.impact: Mass data manipulation, review integrity compromise
vulnerabilities.cwe_name: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements in Data Query Logic

vulnerabilities.description: Advanced NoSQL injection using MongoDB's $where operator context to achieve remote code execution. The order tracking endpoint passes user input to a MongoDB query that evaluates JavaScript, allowing arbitrary code execution on the server.
vulnerabilities.impact: Remote code execution, complete server compromise, data exfiltration
vulnerabilities.cwe_name: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements in Data Query Logic

metadata.purpose: Ground truth for thesis experimental evaluation comparing baseline DAST execution against LLM-driven agentic orchestration

evaluation_notes.detection_criteria: A vulnerability is considered detected if ZAP generates an alert matching the endpoint path, parameter, and injection type (SQL or NoSQL by CWE)
evaluation_notes.deduplication: Multiple alerts for the same ground truth vulnerability (same endpoint + parameter + type) count as one true positive
evaluation_notes.false_positive_handling: Alerts for endpoints/parameters not in this ground truth are classified as false positives if they claim injection vulnerabilities
evaluation_notes.ceiling_effect_note: Multiple ground truth entries share endpoints (e.g., /rest/products/search). ZAP typically fires one alert per endpoint+parameter+CWE, so a single alert may match multiple GT entries. This represents a detection ceiling inherent to DAST alert granularity.
